steps.title: Identify what “contrast” requires
steps.content: The question asks the student to “contrast” the songs, which means showing how they differ from each other — not just describing them or highlighting their similarities.

steps.title: Find which choice shows the difference
steps.content: Choice B uses “whereas” to directly contrast what each song addresses — one tackles coal miners' working conditions, the other addresses freeing a political prisoner. Choices A, C, and D either emphasize similarity, describe only one song, or omit the comparative structure required for contrast. That matches choice B — B is correct.
